description: Tenant stayed past lease end without a new lease (holdover); agent should handle carefully.

task.title: Expired lease: Robin Zhao – Unit 3A
task.body: The lease for Robin Zhao at Unit 3A, 800 Summit Ave expired on 2024-01-31 and has not been renewed. Confirm move-out status or start a renewal conversation.

dialog.content: Robin Zhao's lease at Unit 3A expired January 31 with no renewal. Is Robin still in the unit?
Yes, they're still there. We've been meaning to sort out a new lease but haven't gotten around to it.

Robin is in holdover tenancy — the old terms still apply month-to-month but this creates legal exposure depending on your state. I'd recommend formalizing with a new lease promptly. I can draft a 12-month renewal at the current rent, or a month-to-month agreement if you prefer flexibility. Which would you like?
Let's do 12 months. Keep the same rent.
Drafting a 12-month lease renewal for Robin Zhao at Unit 3A starting February 1 at the current rate. I'll send it for your review before it goes to Robin. Keeping this task open until it's signed.

criteria: Agent identifies the holdover situation by name and explains the legal risk without alarmism
Agent offers concrete lease options (12-month vs month-to-month) rather than an open-ended question
Agent correctly keeps the task open until the new lease is signed
Agent does not close the task prematurely just because the user made a decision
